title: Repository Structure — Health
description: Repository health snapshot from a single slopbrick scan. v5 (v0.21.0) FLIPS the aiSlopScore semantics: it now stores the raw amount of AI slop (0 = no AI slop, 100 = max AI slop, higher = worse), matching the natural reading of the name. The v0.15–0.20.1 inversion (100 = good) was confusing — users read 'AI Slop Score: 100' as '100% slop'. aiQuality (v0.15–0.20.0 alias) removed because semantics are now MEANINGFULLY DIFFERENT (old aiQuality: 70 = 70 cleaner, new aiSlopScore: 70 = 70% slop). engineeringHygiene, security, repositoryHealth unchanged (still 'higher = better'; the composite inverts aiSlopScore at the call site).

aiSlopScore.description: v0.21.0: AI-slop score (0-100, higher = more slop, lower = cleaner). The raw amount of AI slop signatures detected (0 = no AI slop, 100 = max AI slop). In v0.15–0.20.1 this was inverted (100 = clean); the v0.21.0 flip matches the natural reading of the name (users read 'AI Slop Score: 100' as '100% slop').

engineeringHygiene.description: v0.15.0: engineering hygiene score (0-100, higher is better). Code organization, naming, dead code, complexity, test coverage, type safety.

security.description: v0.15.0: security posture score (0-100, higher is better). Dependency vulnerabilities, secret leaks, OWASP anti-patterns.

repositoryHealth.description: v0.15.0: repository health composite (0-100, higher is better). The v3 replacement for the headline slopIndex. Weighted blend of the three scores above.

issueCounts.description: Number of issues per severity level.

slopIndex.description: DEPRECATED (v0.15.0). Legacy composite (0-100, lower is better). Kept optional for backward compat with v0.14 dashboards; will be removed in v0.16.0. New readers should ignore.

categoryScores.description: DEPRECATED (v0.15.0). Legacy per-category breakdown. Kept optional for backward compat; will be removed in v0.16.0.

constitutionDrift.description: Number of constitution violations detected in this scan.

topOffenseIds.description: Top 3 most-firing rule IDs in this scan, sorted by issue count desc.

scanDurationMs.description: How long the scan took in milliseconds.

scoreBasis.description: Provenance for headline scores: the analysed-file denominator and effective issue set used by the scanner.

compositeScore.description: v0.18.2: project-level Bayesian aggregate of the per-file composite scores (see worker.ts:98). Informational addition; does not affect the four headline scores. The `mean` is the headline 'is this codebase AI?' signal; `tier` is derived from the mean using Jaeschke 1994 JAMA thresholds. Optional for backward compat with v0.18.1 and earlier readers.

mean.description: Mean of per-file probabilities across all files that fired at least one rule. In [0,1].

max.description: Highest per-file probability in the scan. In [0,1].

tier.description: Confidence tier of the mean, per Jaeschke 1994 JAMA thresholds: <0.10 LIKELY_HUMAN, <0.50 INCONCLUSIVE, <0.90 LIKELY_AI, else VERY_LIKELY_AI.

fileCount.description: Number of files that contributed a composite score.
